hfToken.description: Hugging Face token for model loading

cpus.description: Number of CPUs to allocate to each worker

cudaGpuMemPercentage.description: Percentage of GPU memory to allocate to each worker

gpus.description: Number of GPUs to use per instance replica

memory.description: Memory in GB to allocate to each worker

modelNameOverride.description: Override the model name for display purposes

initial_replicas.description: Initial number of model replicas to deploy

min_replicas.description: Minimum model replicas to deploy at any given point (can be 0)

max_replicas.description: Maximum model replicas to deploy at any given point

target_ongoing_requests.description: Scale the number of replicas for a deployment up or down based on the average number of ongoing requests per replica.

upscale_delay_s.description: This defines how long Serve waits before scaling up the number of replicas in your deployment.

downscale_delay_s.description: This defines how long Serve waits before scaling down the number of replicas in your deployment.

downscale_to_zero_delay_s.description: This defines how long Serve waits before scaling from one replica down to zero (only applies when min_replicas = 0).

upscaling_factor.description: The multiplicative factor to amplify or moderate each upscaling decision. For example, when the application has high traffic volume in a short period of time, you can increase upscaling_factor to scale up the resource quickly.

downscaling_factor.description: The multiplicative factor to amplify or moderate each downscaling decision. For example, if you want your application to be less sensitive to drops in traffic and scale down more conservatively, you can decrease downscaling_factor to slow down the pace of downscaling.

metrics_interval_s.description: This controls how often each replica and handle sends reports on current ongoing requests to the autoscaler.

look_back_period_s.description: This is the window over which the average number of ongoing requests per replica is calculated.

aggregation_function.description: This controls how metrics are aggregated over the look_back_period_s time window. The aggregation function determines how Ray Serve combines multiple metric measurements into a single value for autoscaling decisions. Supported values: 'mean' (default): Uses time-weighted average of metrics. This provides smooth scaling behavior that responds to sustained traffic patterns. 'max': Uses the maximum metric value observed. This makes autoscaling more sensitive to spikes, scaling up quickly when any replica experiences high load. 'min': Uses the minimum metric value observed. This results in more conservative scaling behavior.

enabled.description: Enable ingress for all ray endpoints

cpus.description: Number of CPUs to allocate to the head node

memory.description: Memory in GB to allocate to the head node

baseUrl.description: Base URL of the LiteLLM service (central model registry). This can be an external LiteLLM instance or an internal deployment in the pool (default)

key.description: Master key of the LiteLLM service (central model registry)

accessGroup.description: What access group to assign the model to (ringfencing)

kalavaiExtras.description: Extra information to store in the LiteLLM model registration (must be a dictionary)

waitReady.description: Wait for the model to be ready before registering it in LiteLLM

envFromSecrets.description: Dictionary mapping environment variable names to secret names

priorityClassName.description: PriorityClassName to use for the deployment

nodeSelectors.description: target specific devices. Expected format -> key: value pairs, where 'value' is a list of elements

nodeSelectorsOps.description: Logical operation to use to apply to nodeSelectors. Accepted values: 'OR', 'AND'

nodeAffinityPreferenceKey.description: Key to use for node affinity
